content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be repurposed as a dynamic, experience-updated search tree for Task and Motion Planning (TAMP), where episodic memory traces of previously executed motion segments replace manually engineered sampling heuristics. Curiosity-driven memory retention mechanisms from modern active learning agents automatically prioritize storing high-uncertainty motion interaction outcomes that are most likely to improve future TAMP generalization to novel environments.
